title: OSF SM Profile — next/ unified meta-schema (ONE standard for machine | equipment | business) — v2.0 (v2.0: one entity-profile standard; OPC binding nested under isa95; empty semantics allowed)
description: Single source of truth for every next/profiles/**/*.json EXCEPT intelligence/ (which keeps the canonical validation/intelligence-profile-schema.json). Merges the formerly-separate machine-/equipment-/business-profile-schema.json (which were ~90% identical) into one base + draft-07 if/then branches keyed on the now-REQUIRED 'category'. The base accepts the UNION of properties all three categories use, so additionalProperties:false still passes every existing next/ profile. Skeleton-first: every build-later block may be EMPTY (attributes [], relationships [], constraints {}, kpiRefs [], semantics absent) so a customer can commit an identity-only skeleton and fill it in later. Cross-reference resolution (predicate.attr -> attributes[].name; predicate.value type vs attribute.dataType) is still enforced separately by next/ci/lint-constraints.mjs (JSON Schema cannot express it); category<->folder placement by next/ci/lint-category.mjs.

_comment.description: Optional doc/meta key (e.g. demo/example/skeleton fixtures). Ignored by consumers.

_example.description: Optional meta flag: marks this as an example/demo/skeleton fixture, not a canonical profile.

category.description: REQUIRED. Selects the per-category if/then branch. 'machine' = OT asset profile (edge-polled OPC). 'equipment' = ISA-88 physical equipment master-data anchor. 'business' = IT/MOM information object (it-edge-polled). intelligence/ profiles use a different (canonical) schema and are out of scope here.

attributes.description: Skeleton-first: [] is valid (no minItems). The union of attribute facets across all three categories; per-category extras (polling for machine; isKey/isId for equipment+business) are gated by the if/then branches below.

promotion.description: Emission cadence (edge-agnostic; the IT and OT edges honor it identically). Kept enum: raw | aggregate | on_change | on_cycle_end | on_event | never. PLUS interval tokens matching ^[0-9]+(sec|min)$ (canonical Nsec/Nmin, N>=1) for periodic emission. never = stays in the payload/snapshot but is excluded from the change-trigger and never published on its own.

polling.description: machine-category only (gated by the if/then branch) — OPC poll mode (e.g. 'scan').

constraints.description: Merged standard: array of constraint objects (each with a stable 'name'). Consistent with attributes[]/relationships[]. Optional/next-facet; [] or absent is valid.

isa95.description: ISA-95/MOM/ISA-88 mapping. Loosely typed on purpose — the equipment/operations model is evolving; deep semantics are reviewed (lint), not schema-pinned. The 'objectModel' key is REQUIRED for category=equipment|business (gated by the if/then branches); for category=machine it is optional.

allOf.comment: machine attributes must not use the equipment/business isKey/isId facets.
equipment & business: require isa95.objectModel; allow isKey/isId on attributes; forbid the machine-only OPC facets.

counter.description:
CAPT-ZUG4 (2026-07-12) — READING SEMANTICS for an attribute whose value is a RUNNING TOTAL, not a measurement. Absent = the value is a measurement and may be averaged. Present = it may NOT: mean/sigma/Cp over a counter are arithmetic on a ramp, and min/max over a counter silently hand the caller `max - min`, which is the wrong answer the moment the counter resets.

WHY THIS IS A CONTRACT FIELD AND NOT A COMMENT. Until now the only place this fact was written down was the attribute's `description` prose ('Good parts produced (cumulative).'). No consumer can read prose. That is EXACTLY the defect that killed qty_shortfall: its guard literal 'offen' was copied from a description that had been invented rather than measured, the code did the opposite of the stated intent, and the rule shipped 11.196 phantom findings and EUR 1.737.072 of phantom impact. A semantic that only humans can see is a semantic the machine will get wrong. So it moves into the contract, where the linter and the edge can both read it.

MEASURED, not assumed (sgm-004 edge TS `uns_history`, full history to 2026-07-12): the sim does not persist its counters, so every container restart sets them back to 0. `good` reset 10 times. Read naively, max-min says 55.622 parts. Read as the sum of positive increments it is 125.210 — the naive answer loses 56 % of the factory's output. The reset-aware reading is confirmed by an INDEPENDENT identity that only holds if it is right: good 125.210 + scrap 1.742 = 126.952 = total 126.952 = 8 cavities x shotCount 15.869, exactly.

semantics.description: cumulative_resettable = a running total that CAN return to zero (device restart, shift/tool reset, 32-bit wrap). cumulative_monotonic = a running total that provably never resets (assert this only if measured — a wrong assertion here is a silent 2x error). delta = the value is already the increment for its interval and must simply be summed. unreadable = it IS a running total, but which entity it belongs to is NOT established, so no reading is correct yet; pair it with aggregation:'refuse'. 'unreadable' is a first-class, honest state — it exists so that 'we do not know' can be SAID in the contract instead of being papered over with a plausible default.

aggregation.description: How a consumer MUST turn samples of this attribute into a quantity over a window. sum_of_positive_deltas = sum(greatest(v - lag(v), 0)) — the only reading that survives a reset. last_minus_first = valid ONLY for cumulative_monotonic. sum = valid only for `delta` semantics. refuse = the engine MUST NOT aggregate this attribute at all and must report an evidence gap instead of a number. A refusal that is written down is worth more than a number nobody can defend.

resetsObserved.description: Evidence, not configuration: how many resets were actually MEASURED in the historian at the date named in `measuredAt`. 0 with semantics=cumulative_resettable is legitimate (it can reset, it just has not yet); it is not a licence to switch to last_minus_first.

measuredAt.description: When resetsObserved was measured. An unmeasured claim is prose again.

quality.description: Optional next/-only attribute facet (FR-SDC2). OPC-UA StatusCode/Quality propagation policy — today the source StatusCode is discarded at the edge. 'onBad' = edge behaviour when source quality is not Good: drop|flag|hold; 'nullFlavor' = HL7/ISO null-flavor token. Optional everywhere; absent = legacy forward-as-is.

semantics.description: Optional next/-only facet (FR-SDC3). IRI reference(s) grounding this profile/attribute in an external vocabulary (CESMII SM, ISO/IEC, BFO, …) for KG-Builder node enrichment. Single prefixed IRI string, array of them, or object form ({ $ref, sameAs[] }). Skeleton-first: absent is valid.
